steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The setup: P6 actually gave equal ratings to abstract and cubist paintings. The graph shows the correlation between the model's predicted ratings and participants' actual ratings for each style. A low correlation for P6 on abstract and cubist means the model's predictions did not track P6's actual ratings well — so even though P6 rated them equally, the model would have predicted different values for those two styles.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'P6's rating for abstract and cubist paintings would equal one another.': That is what P6 actually reported, not what the model predicted. A low model correlation means the model's predictions diverge from P6's ratings. ✗
  • Choice B — 'P6's ratings for abstract and cubist paintings would differ from one another.': Because the model correlates poorly with P6 for these styles, its predicted ratings diverge from the equal actual ratings — the model predicts different values even though P6 gave equal ones. ✓
  • Choice C — 'P6 would derive less aesthetic pleasure from abstract paintings than from cubist paintings.': The graph shows direction of correlation, not the specific ranking; the data do not support this directional claim. ✗
  • Choice D — 'P6 would derive more aesthetic pleasure from abstract paintings than from cubist paintings.': Same problem as C — the graph does not indicate which style the model rated higher for P6. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
